title: Run gofmt only on Go 1.10
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

I think this will work to run gofmt only when go is version 1.10.  I'd like to have this in travis.yml, but couldn't figure a clean way to do so.  Any other suggestions/comments gratefully accepted.

Once working, this should cure our go 1.11 CI issues.

comments.body:
This is failing with a syntax error, I've gone through a number of loops.  @rhatdan or @nalind any thoughts on what bit is out of place?

```
HEAD is now at 2c632d1a Merge pull request #1824 from cyphar/fix-mips-build-devNumber
ln -sf ../../opencontainers/runc/runc
# Run gofmt only on version 1.10
ifneq (,)
/bin/sh: 1: Syntax error: word unexpected (expecting ")")
make: *** [validate] Error 2
```
@q384566678 tried to get the code happy for golang 1.11, but that ended up not working.  I'll try working from that angle too.
Ugh, I love make, the issue was the if statement was indented, it needed to be all the way to the left otherwise make treats it like a shell script.

:pushpin: Commit af73ddd has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan af73dddff357e113ef4754bed36a650229a1fcd3 -->
:zap: Test exempted: pull fully rebased and already tested.
